title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 22, 2026: Tech Rally Fuels Broad Market Gains Amid Earnings Focus

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally as Tech and Semiconductor Shares Lead the Charge
items.summary: Wall Street closed in positive territory today, with all three major U.S. stock indices recording gains. The broad-based rally was primarily driven by strength in the technology and semiconductor sectors, as investors focused on the robust start to the second-quarter earnings season and expressed optimism surrounding artificial intelligence. Geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices were largely overlooked as market participants returned to growth stocks.

items.headline: SanDisk Surges on Semiconductor Rally
items.summary: SanDisk led the market's gains today, benefiting from a sharp rebound in semiconductor stocks driven by optimism around artificial intelligence.

items.headline: Western Digital Rises Amid Chip Sector Strength
items.summary: Western Digital advanced as the semiconductor sector staged a powerful recovery.

items.headline: Micron Technology Leads Memory Chip Gains
items.summary: Micron Technology jumped, leading memory chip stocks higher, fueled by robust performance expectations for chip sales and AI optimism.

items.headline: Seagate Technology Climbs on Tech-Led Rally
items.summary: Seagate Technology saw significant gains as technology stocks broadly outperformed.

items.headline: Hasbro Boosted by Upbeat Forecast
items.summary: Hasbro's stock climbed sharply after the company raised its annual revenue and profit forecasts.

items.headline: Intel Performs Strongly on Chip Sales Optimism
items.summary: Intel experienced robust performance following positive expectations for chip sales, aligning with broader AI sentiment.

items.headline: Danaher Falls on Trimmed Outlook
items.summary: Danaher's shares sank after the life sciences firm trimmed its core revenue growth outlook and reported weaker-than-expected revenue in its biotechnology business.

items.headline: Technology Leads as Consumer Staples Lag in Sector Performance
items.summary: Information Technology was the leading sector today, gaining 2.4%, with semiconductor-related ETFs such as VanEck Semiconductor ETF ($SMH) surging 4% and iShares Semiconductor ETF ($SOXX) adding 5.5%. Conversely, Consumer Staples lagged, declining 1%. This indicates a rotation into growth stocks, particularly within tech, as investors seek AI-driven opportunities.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports on Deck; Capital One, Chubb Beat Estimates
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season continues to be a focal point, with several major technology companies, including Alphabet ($GOOGL), Tesla ($TSLA), IBM ($IBM), and Texas Instruments ($TXN), scheduled to report their quarterly results later today or tomorrow. Earlier, Capital One Financial and Chubb reported earnings that surpassed expectations. General Motors ($GM) also climbed on better-than-expected earnings.

items.headline: Rising Oil Prices and Geopolitical Tensions Create Macro Headwinds
items.summary: Brent crude oil prices climbed above $92 a barrel today, fueled by renewed geopolitical tensions, particularly the re-escalation of the U.S.-Iran conflict. These rising energy costs are reviving inflation concerns and could complicate the economic outlook, despite recent softer CPI and PPI reports. In currency markets, the Japanese yen slumped to a 40-year low against the dollar, trading above 163.00.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady Amid Moderating Inflation Signals
items.summary: While there were no major Federal Reserve announcements today, market expectations suggest the Fed will likely keep interest rates unchanged at its upcoming July meeting. This follows recent June data indicating a meaningful cooling in both the Consumer Price Index and Producer Price Index. The labor market remains broadly stable.
